project: Hospital Operations & Revenue Risk Intelligence Platform

model_a.name: Visit Risk Classifier
model_a.task: 3-class classification: Low / Medium / High visit risk
model_a.intended_use: Support clinical staff in proactively prioritising High-Risk visits for resource allocation

fairness.gender_disparity: <0.01 F1 gap between Female and Male — acceptable
fairness.city_disparity: Hyderabad shows slightly lower F1 — monitor post-deployment

known_limitations: Significant overfitting: train accuracy 70.9% vs test accuracy 41.1% — model has memorised training patterns
Low recall for 'High' risk class (11.6%) — model misses many High-Risk visits
risk_score in source data has inherent variability not fully explained by available features
Improvement path: add clinical features (diagnosis codes, vitals), tune max_depth, or use XGBoost
assumptions: Patient registration date is available at time of visit
Department and visit_type are known at admission
Patient historical visit frequency is available in real time
model_a.retraining_strategy: Retrain monthly with rolling 12-month window; trigger immediate retraining if test F1 drops below 0.28

model_b.name: Claim Outcome Classifier
model_b.algorithm: Random Forest (100 trees, max_depth=12) + SMOTE oversampling
model_b.task: 3-class classification: Paid / Pending / Rejected claim outcome
model_b.intended_use: Predict claim rejection risk before submission, enabling finance teams to fix claims proactively
model_b.training_period: Earliest 80% of billing records by date
model_b.imbalance_strategy: SMOTE oversampling (k_neighbors=5) to balance 3 classes before training

fairness.insurance_disparity: All providers within 0.016 F1 of each other — no significant disparity

known_limitations: FEATURE LEAKAGE RISK: approval_ratio and approved_amount may not be available before claim submission
Production deployment must replace these with historical averages per provider/department
Without leaky features: accuracy drops to ~60% — still useful but significantly weaker
SMOTE creates synthetic samples — model may be overconfident on minority classes
assumptions: Historical approval ratios by provider and department are available as lookup tables
Billing amounts are determined before claim submission
Insurance provider is known at time of visit
model_b.retraining_strategy: Retrain quarterly; trigger retraining if recall for Rejected drops below 0.85 or if a new insurance provider is onboarded
